_template_note: MCP server catalogue. All servers default to enabled:false — flip the ones you want on. Most use stdio transport; fill in tokens via env vars at launch time (NEVER commit real tokens here). Naming follows kebab-case per .claude/rules/mcp-conventions.md. The _group field is purely for UI grouping and is ignored at runtime.

project-tools._comment: Placeholder for this project's own HTTP-transport MCP server (if you build one)

rain-docs._comment: rain internal software-engineering docs (software-engineering.docs.rain.co.za). 1,720 pages across 11+ sections. Tools: rain_search, rain_get_page, rain_list_sections, rain_list_pages_in_section.

filesystem._comment: Official. Read/write/search a sandboxed directory on disk. Set FILESYSTEM_ROOT.
args: ${FILESYSTEM_ROOT:-C:/Users/BaptistaManuel/Downloads/Process Automation Specialist/Master File for all projects}

fetch._comment: Official. Fetch URLs and convert HTML → markdown. No auth required.

context7._comment: Upstash Context7. Up-to-date library/framework docs on demand. Free tier, no auth.

sequential-thinking._comment: Official. Structured step-by-step reasoning. No auth.

memory._comment: Official. Persistent knowledge graph across sessions. Stores in ~/.memory.

memory-bank._comment: Community memory-bank MCP (alioshr). Per-project markdown files under MEMORY_BANK_ROOT — human-readable companion to the knowledge-graph 'memory' entry.

github._comment: GitHub's official MCP. Fine-grained PAT with repo + issues + actions scopes.

gitlab._comment: Community GitLab MCP. Set GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN + GITLAB_API_URL.

atlassian._comment: Atlassian Remote MCP — Jira + Confluence. OAuth in-browser.

playwright._comment: Microsoft-maintained Playwright MCP. Full browser control.

puppeteer._comment: Official Puppeteer MCP. Lighter than Playwright for quick automation.

browser-use._comment: Community. High-level 'do this in a browser' agent. Form-filling etc.

postgres._comment: Official. Read-only Postgres queries via DATABASE_URL.

supabase._comment: Supabase official MCP. DB + auth + storage over your project ref.

sentry._comment: Sentry's official remote MCP. Issue triage. OAuth via browser.

notion._comment: Notion's official MCP. Internal integration token + page share.

google-drive._comment: Google Drive (Google's new MCP suite). OAuth via gdrive-credentials.

gmail._comment: Community Gmail MCP. OAuth device flow on first run.

google-maps._comment: Official Google Maps MCP. Places API key required.

apidog._comment: Apidog MCP. Bridges an OpenAPI/Apidog spec into Claude so it can generate clients, docs, and tests from the spec. Point APIDOG_OAS_URL at your spec URL or local path.

figma._comment: Figma's official Dev Mode MCP. Needs Dev Mode + Figma desktop on port 3845.

shadcn._comment: Shadcn Registry MCP — browse/install shadcn/ui components.

clickup._comment: Community ClickUp MCP. Workspace 90121466494, team pre-configured.

linear._comment: Linear's official remote MCP. OAuth on first use.

asana._comment: Community Asana MCP. Personal access token required.

huggingface._comment: Hugging Face official MCP. Model + dataset + spaces search.

exa._comment: Exa AI search — semantic web search tuned for LLM agents.

brave-search._comment: Official Brave Search MCP. Free tier with BRAVE_API_KEY.

firecrawl._comment: Firecrawl MCP — reliable web scraping + crawling.

docker._comment: Community Docker MCP. Manage containers, images, networks. Requires daemon.

kubernetes._comment: Kubernetes MCP. Reads current kubecontext; set KUBECONFIG.

cloudflare._comment: Cloudflare's official remote MCP. DNS, KV, R2, Workers. OAuth.

aws._comment: AWS Labs core MCP (STS, S3, Lambda, CloudWatch).

slack._comment: Official Slack MCP. Bot token (xoxb-...) + team id.

discord._comment: Community Discord MCP. Complements the /chat Discord bot.

stripe._comment: Stripe's official MCP. Read-only by default; STRIPE_MODE=write to mutate.

zapier._comment: Zapier's official remote MCP. OAuth in-browser on first use. Exposes the user's existing Zaps as callable tools.
